main.name: main
main.description: General-purpose agent, using the currently active profile

direct.name: direct
direct.description: Direct-interaction agent, does not use artifact file

direct-web.name: direct-web
direct-web.description: Direct-interaction agent, with web system prompt

direct-review.name: direct-review
direct-review.description: Direct-interaction review agent, does not use artifact file

scheduler-main.name: scheduler-main
scheduler-main.description: Scheduled task agent, executes scheduled tasks

planner.name: planner
planner.description: Planner — draft plans and write to artifact

reviewer.name: reviewer
reviewer.description: Reviewer — evaluate artifact content and give feedback, end with [APPROVED] or list Blockers
reviewer.directive: You are a strict reviewer. Evaluate the plan for correctness, completeness, and feasibility.
reviewer.promptTemplate: Read the content in {{artifactPath}} and perform a review. Append your review to the ## Review section at the end of the file. If the content passes, write [APPROVED] at the end of the review. Otherwise provide specific suggestions for improvement.

plan-reviewer.name: plan-reviewer
plan-reviewer.description: Plan Reviewer — audit one phase's output against the external plan's acceptance criteria in the plan-gate template, issue Approve / Revise / Reject on the last line
plan-reviewer.promptTemplate:
Execute the plan-phase audit per the plan-reviewer directive.

Task / context (must declare plan path, phase under review, and where the phase's artifacts live):
{{input}}

---

Write the review to {{artifactPath}}. Walk every acceptance criterion of the phase explicitly (met / partially met / not met with a citation). End the artifact with a single line in the exact form: `Verdict: Approve | Revise | Reject` (one word, no modifiers).

plan-executor.name: plan-executor
plan-executor.description: Plan Executor — translate Plan Reviewer's verdict into task creation in the plan-gate template (Approve→next phase / Revise→patch+retry gate / Reject→stop), do not modify plan file structure
plan-executor.promptTemplate:
Translate Plan Reviewer's verdict into operations per the plan-executor directive.

Plan Reviewer's report (previous step):
{{previousOutput}}

---

Original task / context:
{{input}}

---

Reviewer's full artifact is at {{artifactPath}} — read it end-to-end first, extract the `Verdict:` word, then run the matching branch (Approve / Revise / Reject). All new tasks MUST carry `template:plan-gate` and full metadata; gate tasks always start `PLAN-GATE: `. Do NOT modify the plan file's structure or acceptance criteria.

milestone-reviewer.name: milestone-reviewer
milestone-reviewer.description: Milestone Reviewer — execute adversarial review in the milestone-gate template, check milestone output against roadmap.md success conditions, output Blocker / Nice-to-have, do not issue verdict (Director is responsible)
milestone-reviewer.promptTemplate:
Execute the milestone adversarial audit per the milestone-reviewer directive.

Task / context:
{{input}}

---

Write the review to {{artifactPath}}. Cover every dimension the milestone's success criteria can be judged on. Each issue must carry a citation and a Blocker / Nice-to-have label. Do NOT issue a Director-style verdict — that belongs to Director.

director.name: director
director.description: Project Director — consume Milestone Reviewer report in the milestone-gate template and issue Proceed / Iterate / Pivot / Abort verdict; write result to artifact
director.promptTemplate:
Execute the milestone verdict per the director directive.

Milestone Reviewer's report (previous step):
{{previousOutput}}

---

Original task / context:
{{input}}

---

Reviewer's full artifact is at {{artifactPath}} — read it end-to-end first. Write your analysis and the final `Verdict:` line back to the same artifact. Artifact MUST agree on verdict word and top reasons.

milestone-executor.name: milestone-executor
milestone-executor.description: Milestone Executor — translate Director's verdict into roadmap updates and task creation in the milestone-gate template, all writes go to project files
milestone-executor.promptTemplate:
Translate Director's verdict into operations per the milestone-executor directive.

Director's analysis (previous step):
{{previousOutput}}

---

Original task / context:
{{input}}

---

Director's full artifact is at {{artifactPath}} — read it end-to-end first and extract the `Verdict:` word. Then execute the matching branch (Proceed / Iterate / Pivot / Abort) per directive.

doc-writer.name: doc-writer
doc-writer.description: Doc Writer — general document producer, outputs structured documents such as status / digest / decision / report / knowledge entry, writes to project files

write.description: Initial writing pass — produce or update the target document, then summarize changes in the artifact.

retry.description: After a blocking `## Review`: fix and append `## Write Summary (iteration N)` + `[REVISED]`.
retry.promptTemplate: The Reviewer's `## Review` has been written to {{artifactPath}}. First verify each Blocker against its cited source — the Reviewer is not infallible. Then fix the document files per the feedback. When done, append `## Write Summary (iteration N)` and MUST write `[REVISED]` to {{artifactPath}} on its own line.

doc-reviewer.name: doc-reviewer
doc-reviewer.description: Doc Reviewer — audit Doc Writer's changes to project documents, grade by original requirements + Blocker / Nice-to-have

coder.name: coder
coder.description: Coder — code implementation executor. Works in multiple stages in the coder-review template: plan → implement → retry, driven by transition graph rather than artifact section inference

plan.description: Draft the implementation plan under `## Plan (iteration 1)`.

implement.description: After `## Plan Review`: exit plan mode, implement, append `## Implementation Summary (iteration 1)`.
implement.promptTemplate: Begin implementation. When done, append `## Implementation Summary (iteration 1)` to the end of the artifact, listing changed files, commit short-SHAs, flagged ambiguities, env changes, and accept/reject with rationale for each plan-review item. Do NOT write `[IMPL-APPROVED]` — only the reviewer writes that. After committing, run the full test suite (`npm test` or equivalent) and confirm every stage passes before writing your summary.

retry.description: After a blocking `## Impl Review`: fix and append `## Implementation Summary (iteration 2)` + `[REVISED]`.
retry.promptTemplate: The latest `## Impl Review` did not provide `[IMPL-APPROVED]`. Re-verify each Blocker against its cited source, then fix the code per the feedback. When finished revising, MUST write `[REVISED]`  to {{artifactPath}} on its own line.

coder-reviewer.name: coder-reviewer
coder-reviewer.description: Coder Reviewer — works in two stages (planReview / implReview) in the coder-review template, transition graph determines stage rather than artifact inference

planReview.description: Read `## Plan` and append `## Plan Review (iteration N)` — no approval marker here.

implReview.description: Read `## Implementation Summary` + diff and append `## Impl Review (iteration M)` — must end with `[IMPL-APPROVED]` or list Blockers.
implReview.promptTemplate:
Audit Coder's project-file changes (changes by other agents in the same files may appear — ignore those):
{{modifiedFiles}}

Read the latest `## Implementation Summary (iteration M)` in {{artifactPath}} (and the following `## Response to Impl Review` if present). Append `## Impl Review (iteration M)` to {{artifactPath}}. **Remember: you MUST write your review to {{artifactPath}}.** If approved, end with `[IMPL-APPROVED]`; otherwise list the Blockers. Coder gets only 1 retry after the first block; beyond that the pipeline ends. Do not fabricate approval markers.

Before writing your review, run the project's full test suite (`npm test` or equivalent). Check that every stage passes: architecture linter, unit tests, integration tests. Any failure is a Blocker — do not write [IMPL-APPROVED] until the full suite is green.

executor.name: executor
executor.description: Executor — general task executor, performs assigned work (edit files, run commands, modify code, config changes, etc.), writes results to artifact. Stages: execute → retry

execute.description: Execute the task, write ## Execute Summary to artifact

retry.description: Address reviewer Blockers, write [REVISED] after fixing
retry.promptTemplate: The Reviewer's `## Review` has been written to {{artifactPath}}. First verify each Blocker against its cited source — the Reviewer is not infallible. Fix the issues per the feedback. When done, append `## Execute Summary (iteration N)` and MUST write `[REVISED]` to {{artifactPath}} on its own line.

executor-reviewer.name: executor-reviewer
executor-reviewer.description: Executor Reviewer — audit Executor's execution results, check correctness, completeness, and safety against task requirements + diff

default.name: default
default.description: Single agent with file workspace; entry agent resolves to current !agent

scheduler.name: scheduler
scheduler.description: Scheduled task — single agent + onEnd hook (compound + git commit)

plan-review.name: plan-review
plan-review.description: Plan-then-review (no execution): planner drafts → reviewer audits → END. Use this when you want a multi-step plan adversarially reviewed before committing to execution.

doc-review.name: doc-review
doc-review.description: Generic produce-then-audit for documents (status / digest / decision / report / knowledge entry). Stages: doc-writer(write) → doc-reviewer → (if not [APPROVED]) doc-writer(retry, write [REVISED] at end) → END

coder-review.name: coder-review
coder-review.description: Stage-driven coder loop: coder(plan) → coder(implement) → reviewer(implReview) → (if implReview lacks [IMPL-APPROVED]) coder(retry, write [REVISED] at end) → END. Each stage's prompt is selected by the transition graph in thread-engine, no longer inferred from artifact sections.

plan-gate.name: plan-gate
plan-gate.description: Plan-phase gate: plan-reviewer audits one phase against the externally-supplied plan's acceptance criteria and issues Approve / Revise / Reject; plan-executor dispatches the matching branch (next-phase tasks / patch tasks / stop). Does NOT touch roadmap.md or plan-file structure.

milestone-gate.name: milestone-gate
milestone-gate.description: Milestone evaluation: adversarial review → strategic verdict (Proceed/Iterate/Pivot/Abort) → operational follow-up. milestone-reviewer audits milestone output → director issues verdict → milestone-executor writes roadmap / creates next-phase tasks. Suitable for phase decisions in any mission-driven project.

execute-review.name: execute-review
execute-review.description: Execute-then-review: executor executes task → executor-reviewer audits → at most 1 retry. Suitable for any verifiable task (code changes, config changes, file edits, script execution, etc.)
